_comment: CS2 Docs MCP Server egg for Pterodactyl. Pair with the image built from pterodactyl/Dockerfile.pterodactyl. Set up a daily 04:00 schedule with a 'Send command' task of payload 'update-docs' to auto-refresh docs (reindex happens inline, no restart needed).
name: CS2 Docs MCP Server
author: shmitzas@example.com
description: Remote MCP server (FastMCP over SSE) that indexes CS2-related markdown docs — SwiftlyS2, Source 2, GameTracking-CS2, and friends — and exposes search/browse tools to MCP clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and VS Code Copilot. Includes daily update scripts for swiftlys2, source2, and GameTracking-CS2 — trigger with the 'update-docs' console command (or a daily 04:00 schedule) and the index hot-reloads without restarting.

installation.script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e
mkdir -p /mnt/server/docs /mnt/server/.cache
echo 'Install complete. On first start, run the console command:  update-docs'
echo 'to populate docs/. Or upload markdown files under /home/container/docs/ via the File Manager.'

variables.name: Port
variables.description: TCP port the MCP server listens on. Must match the primary allocation port assigned to this server.

variables.name: Max Concurrent Requests
variables.description: Semaphore limit on in-flight MCP tool invocations. Raise for busier deployments; default is fine for personal use.

variables.name: File Cache Size
variables.description: Max number of document file contents kept in memory (LRU). Larger = faster repeat reads, more RAM.

variables.name: Result Cache TTL
variables.description: Seconds that identical MCP tool call results are memoised. Set to 0 to disable.

variables.name: Cleanup Interval
variables.description: Seconds between internal cache cleanup passes. Increase on tight-memory nodes.
